# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  require 'tempfile'

  def upload
    uploaded_io = params[:image_file]

    temp_file_name = Rails.root.join('tmp', 'uploads', uploaded_io.original_filename)
    File.open(temp_file_name, 'wb') do |file|
      file.write(uploaded_io.read)
    end

    convert_image(temp_file_name, params[:format], params[:size])

    # assuming the file path is relevant to `public`
    render json: { status: 'success', image_path: "/temp/#{temp_file_name}" }
  end

  private

  def convert_image(image_path, format, size)
    converted_path = image_path.gsub(/\.\w+$/, ".#{format}")
    convert_command = "convert #{image_path} -resize #{size} #{converted_path}"

    system(convert_command)
    File.delete(image_path) # Delete the original image after conversion
  end
end
